title: Prompt Security, Untrusted Context, Privacy, and Authorization · Production Prompt Mission
summary: A research operations group. The group needs sourced research across untrusted documents without unauthorized actions or instruction injection. Planned artifact: Deliver Secure Tool-Using Research Prompt with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Base the next decision on actual tool results and distinguish inference from observation.
Require approval for consequential, external, destructive, or authority-expanding actions.
Separate trusted instructions from untrusted data and label authority boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
project case — Use a changed project case for prompt security injection that tests this competency: defend the module contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

stimulus.title: Red Team Maintenance failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: required

options.text: Keep the plausible Red Team Maintenance output and hide the violated a prompt threat model and red-team suite assumption instead of explaining the failure. This does not resolve test an acceptance boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, apply Red Team Maintenance without its stated preconditions, omit the a prompt threat model and red-team suite trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Red Team Maintenance for a prompt threat model and red-team suite.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Red Team Maintenance condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the stakeholder delivery
steps.instruction: Write the project evidence note for Red Team Maintenance: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Use a changed project case for prompt security injection that tests this competency: Maintain adversarial cases and re-run them when prompts, models, tools, or data change.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
steps.why: Retrieving Red Team Maintenance through the concrete a prompt threat model and red-team suite case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Maintain adversarial cases and re-run them when prompts, models, tools, or data change.
